reviews.review_title: This game is trash and nobody sees it.
reviews.review_description:
It’s not scary ￼in any way, the train in stump and any rope in the game crashes your game and probably more things crash your game but I don’t know because ￼￼￼I stopped playing in the first 5 minutes. And the obvious… ITS JUST GORILLA TAG BUT ITS WORSE AND IT HAS 2 EQUALLY WORSE MOD MENUS.

And that’s all I wanted to say. Oh and one more thing, DONT PLAY THIS GARBAGE, goodbye.

If you're a fan of VR multiplayer games, Lucky Horror is an exciting addition to the genre. Drawing heavy inspiration from Gorilla Tag, it manages to offer a similar chase-and-avoid dynamic, but with a spine-chilling horror twist that sets it apart.

In Lucky Horror, players take on the role of either a relentless "monster" or a terrified "survivor." The survivors navigate dark and eerie environments while being hunted by the monster. The game’s mechanics, which rely on physical movement and VR controls, create a sense of immersion that is both exhilarating and terrifying.

Gameplay & Mechanics: The gameplay is simple, yet engaging. The survivors must use their wits and speed to evade the monster, who has superior vision but must rely on sound and environmental cues to locate their prey. The chase is intense, and the horror elements, like sudden jump scares, keep you on edge, heightening the tension of the game.

The physicality of moving around like in Gorilla Tag brings a fresh experience to the horror genre. The movement feels responsive, and while you're being chased, the frantic nature of VR adds to the thrill. The environments are beautifully eerie, with dark corners, haunting sounds, and atmospheric settings that keep you constantly looking over your shoulder.

Visuals & Atmosphere: The game features atmospheric settings with dark, ominous visuals that add to the immersion. From abandoned buildings to creepy forests, the environments are designed to make you feel vulnerable, heightening the sense of fear.

The lighting and shadows play an important role in keeping the survivors in constant suspense, as they never know when the monster might pounce. The sound design is fantastic, with eerie noises and distant growls that keep you on alert. Every footstep and creak makes your heart race as you try to outwit the monster.

Overall Experience: Lucky Horror is a unique and thrilling VR experience that blends the fun of Gorilla Tag with the suspense of a horror game. The combination of physical gameplay, immersive environments, and tense chase sequences makes it a standout title for those who enjoy both horror and VR movement-based games. It offers a fun, nerve-wracking experience with friends, as you work together to survive or hunt in a game that keeps you coming back for more.

For anyone looking for an adrenaline-pumping game that will leave you scared and laughing all at once, Lucky Horror is definitely worth checking out.
